_comment: Content evals for dependency-auditor per agentskills.io spec. Each case runs TWICE: with the skill loaded (with_skill) and without (without_skill). Workspace: dependency-auditor-workspace/iteration-N/<case-id>/{with_skill,without_skill}/{outputs,timing.json,grading.json}. Assertions added AFTER first run.

cases.prompt: Run a security audit on zava-storefront/security-fixtures/ and give me a remediation report I can hand to my team. I need to know which findings are safe to bump, which are breaking, and which need manual review.
cases.expected_output: Runs `npm audit --json` (or equivalent), parses the JSON shape, classifies every vulnerability into safe-bump / breaking-bump / fix-via-force / manual-review per the rubric. Emits a markdown report with severity counts, top-5 critical/high in a headline table, and a collapsible full list. Does NOT modify package.json.

assertions.rubric: Are the classifications consistent with the rubric (safe-bump = isSemVerMajor:false; breaking-bump = isSemVerMajor:true; fix-via-force = boolean true; manual-review = false/missing)? PASS only if every classification in the report matches the rubric for the corresponding fixAvailable shape.

cases.prompt: Our security-fixtures app keeps showing CVE warnings — what should we do?
cases.expected_output: Skill activates despite indirect phrasing (no 'audit' verb). Produces structured output grounded in actual `npm audit` data, not fabricated CVE IDs. The without_skill baseline is expected to either skip running npm audit OR hallucinate vulnerability lists from training data — that delta is the evidence the skill adds value.

assertions.rubric: Compare with_skill vs without_skill output blind. Which one would a security engineer actually use to triage? PASS if with_skill is judged grounded in real npm audit output rather than recalled CVE patterns.

cases.prompt: Refactor the auth module in zava-storefront/src/auth/ to use the new session pattern.
cases.expected_output: Skill correctly declines (per 'Do not invoke for: source code changes' clause). Either no-ops or surfaces that this skill is for security audits only and suggests the user invoke a code-refactor skill instead. Does NOT attempt to run npm audit on a refactor request.

assertions.rubric: Did the skill cleanly hand off without running npm audit on an unrelated request? PASS if the decline is explicit.

iteration_workflow._comment: Per agentskills.io: spawn subagent (or clean session) per run. Capture outputs/, timing.json, grading.json. Aggregate to benchmark.json. Iteration 1 explores; iteration 2+ hardens with assertions.
iteration_workflow.ship_gate: All 3 cases PASS with_skill AND show measurable delta vs without_skill. If with_skill ≈ without_skill, the skill is not adding value — redesign or delete.
